name: @forwardimpact/gear
description: Shared libraries and services for platform builders and agents — CLIs, retrieval, evaluation, and infrastructure published to npm.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: Building an agent that needs structured knowledge or typed contracts — and the alternative is reimplementing plumbing from scratch.
jobs.bigHire: give humans and agents shared capabilities through the same interface, with tooling to prove changes improved outcomes.
jobs.littleHire: query graphs, run vector search, or expose services as MCP tools without building infrastructure.
jobs.competesWith: hand-rolled retrieval; bespoke wrappers; ad-hoc frameworks; monolithic platforms

forces.push: Every agent project rebuilds retrieval, transport, and interface plumbing.
forces.pull: Interoperable libraries that work standalone or together.
forces.habit: Copy-pasting infrastructure rather than using shared primitives.
forces.anxiety: Shared libraries mean losing control over the interface contract.

jobs.firedWhen: the libraries diverge from how agents consume them; or a monolithic framework absorbs the surface.

jobs.trigger: An agent change shipped but nobody can tell whether it improved outcomes — the only evidence is anecdotal.
jobs.bigHire: prove whether agent changes improved outcomes with reproducible evidence.
jobs.littleHire: generate test data and chart agent metrics to distinguish signal from noise.
jobs.competesWith: spot-checking; anecdotal feedback; shipping without measuring; custom eval harnesses

forces.push: Agent improvements are claimed but never measured — regressions hide until users complain.
forces.pull: Reproducible evals and control charts that make improvement visible.
forces.habit: Assuming changes helped because the prompt looks better.
forces.anxiety: Rigorous evaluation might reveal that recent changes made things worse.

jobs.firedWhen: eval overhead exceeds the value of knowing; or the team stops iterating on agents.
